behavior_summary: Handles a U8P_LEAPFAIT::hatch/fast-area event for an egg/item: reads the egg id and, depending on that id and two global flags, enables an NPC 'air-walk' mode and sometimes updates the item's quality.

If Egg::getEggId(item) == 0: if global 'stratosMet' is true, enable air-walk using one parameter variant (01,00) on the actor and set the item's quality to 2; otherwise consult 'permissionToAirWalk' and, if that global is zero, enable air-walk with a different parameter variant (01,01).
If Egg::getEggId(item) == 1: perform an inline looped/script sequence of checks on the item (exact checks inferred from loops) and, if those checks pass, enable air-walk with the (01,00) variant.
The function only reads global flags (no globals are written) and returns after taking the appropriate branch(s).
notable_control_flow: Entry: Event 7 / U8P_LEAPFAIT::hatch (fast-area trigger) — U8P_LEAPFAIT::hatch is invoked with an item/egg parameter.
Primary dispatch based on eggId (local eggId at [BP-02h]): branch when eggId == 0, branch when eggId == 1, otherwise no special action.

eggId == 0 branch: nested check of global 'stratosMet' first (true -> air-walk variant (01,00) + Item::setQuality(item,2)); if false, check global 'permissionToAirWalk' and call air-walk variant (01,01) only if permissionToAirWalk == 0.
eggId == 1 branch: run looped/inline script checks (loop/loopscr) using temporary locals; only if those checks succeed call Npc::setAirWalkEnabled(actor, 01,00).
open_questions: What exactly do the numeric byte parameters to Npc::setAirWalkEnabled (e.g., (01,00) vs (01,01)) represent in gameplay terms (mode, duration, permission flag)?
What are the precise conditions evaluated by the loop/loopscr sequence in the eggId == 1 branch (the disassembly shows looped checks but not their semantic intent)?
What is the exact identity and type of the actor pointer pushed from [SP+02h] (inferred to be the NPC/actor to enable air-walk on) and whether it is always safe to call Npc::setAirWalkEnabled on it?

main_function.name: U8P_LEAPFAIT::hatch
main_function.purpose: Respond to a U8P_LEAPFAIT::hatch/fast-area trigger by checking the egg/item id and conditionally enabling an NPC's air-walk ability and adjusting the item's quality based on egg type and global state.
entry_conditions: Event 7 / U8P_LEAPFAIT::hatch (fast-area trigger — invoked when the avatar or relevant actor enters the U8P_LEAPFAIT::hatch/trigger region)

function_map.name: U8P_LEAPFAIT::hatch
function_map.role: Main handler for U8P_LEAPFAIT::hatch/fast-area event that enables air-walk and adjusts item quality based on egg id and globals.

function_map.name: Egg::getEggId
function_map.role: Return an identifier for the passed egg/item; drives branching logic in the main handler.

function_map.name: Npc::setAirWalkEnabled
function_map.role: Enable/configure an NPC's air-walk ability; called with an actor pointer and byte flags (variants used to represent different modes/permissions).

function_map.name: Item::setQuality
function_map.role: Set the quality field on the item instance (used to set quality to 2 in one branch).

external_calls.purpose: Obtain egg/item id for branching decisions (eggId compared to 0 and 1).
Enable or configure air-walk on the relevant actor; called with different byte-parameter variants depending on eggId and globals.
Set the passed item's quality to 2 in the branch where 'stratosMet' is true and eggId == 0.

reads.name: stratosMet
reads.likely_effect: If nonzero, causes the handler to enable air-walk with the (01,00) variant and set the item's quality to 2 when eggId == 0.

reads.name: permissionToAirWalk
reads.likely_effect: When eggId == 0 and stratosMet == 0, if permissionToAirWalk == 0 the handler enables air-walk with the (01,01) variant; if nonzero it skips this call.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles a U8P_LEAPFAIT::hatch/fast-area event for an egg/item: reads the egg id and, depending on that id and two global flags, enables an NPC 'air-walk' mode and sometimes updates the item's quality.
